pr.title: Fix usages of bool as parameter name
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed many fighter item-callback boolean parameters that were literally named `bool`, `bool1`, or generic `arg1` to `flag` across character init C files and matching headers. The PR was a broad naming cleanup with no behavioral changes: function bodies still forward the same value to `Fighter_OnItemPickup`, `Fighter_OnItemDrop`, and related extended item pickup/drop wrappers.
what_changed: Replaced illegal or confusing parameter names such as `bool` and `bool1` with `flag` in numerous `*_Init_OnItemPickup` and `*_Init_OnItemDrop` implementations.
Updated corresponding function prototypes in character init headers so declarations and definitions use the same parameter name.
Renamed some generic `arg1` parameters to `flag` where they are forwarded through item pickup/drop wrapper functions, for example `ftCl_Init_OnItemPickupExt` and `ftCl_Init_OnItemDropExt`.
Preserved function behavior: the renamed boolean is still passed through to calls like `Fighter_OnItemPickup(gobj, flag, 1, 1)` and `Fighter_OnItemDrop(gobj, flag, 1, 1)`.
Touched 46 files under `src/melee/ft/chara/...` plus `src/melee/ft/inlines.h`, with balanced additions/deletions indicating rename-only cleanup.
smart_moves: Used a single neutral replacement name, `flag`, consistently across many near-identical fighter item callback signatures.
Kept prototypes synchronized with definitions, avoiding mismatches between `.c` and `.h` files.
Made a repository-wide cleanup rather than fixing only one fighter, reducing recurring style and compile-compatibility issues in copied item callback patterns.
Avoided changing callback semantics or constants passed to `Fighter_OnItemPickup`, `Fighter_OnItemDrop`, and `Fighter_OnItemVisible`; the diff is easy to audit as naming-only.
decomp_lessons: Do not use C type names or reserved-looking identifiers such as `bool` as parameter names in decomp code, even when the compiler might accept the surrounding signature in some contexts.
For unknown boolean callback parameters, `flag` is an accepted generic placeholder when there is not enough evidence for a semantic name.
When renaming parameters for matching code, update both implementation and header declarations in the same PR to keep generated docs, prototypes, and call sites consistent.
Large mechanical renames are safest when behavior is visibly unchanged and every use of the old parameter in the function body is renamed directly.
Repeated fighter callback patterns can be cleaned uniformly across many character init files; future decomp work should search all analogous fighters before landing a one-off naming fix.
naming_conventions: Use `flag` for unidentified boolean callback parameters instead of `bool`, `bool1`, or `arg1`.
Keep callback names in the existing `ftXX_Init_OnItemPickup`, `ftXX_Init_OnItemDrop`, `ftXX_Init_OnItemInvisible`, and `ftXX_Init_OnItemVisible` style.
Header prototypes may include the parameter name when it clarifies a non-obvious boolean, as in `bool flag`.
Avoid parameter names that duplicate typedef/type names such as `bool`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR did not present new assembly matching tactics; the evidence shows direct parameter renames only.
The `MUST_MATCH` unused stack blocks in affected functions, such as CLink item drop/pickup wrappers, were left intact, showing the cleanup intentionally avoided perturbing matching scaffolding.
Renaming local parameter identifiers is a safe refactor for matching because it does not alter generated code when types, order, and uses are unchanged.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `bool bool` or `bool bool1` parameters outside the fighter character init files.
Search for remaining generic `arg1` boolean parameters in item pickup/drop callbacks that could be safely renamed to `flag` or a more semantic name.
Check whether `dropItemFlag` or other more descriptive names exist for the same callback parameter and should be standardized across fighters.
Audit headers for unnamed boolean parameters where adding `flag` would improve consistency without inventing semantics.
